question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the prune() deletion of ContractResult records by submitting a transaction with a manipulated consensusTimestamp far in the future, causing all historical contract results (including fund-transfer records) to be permanently deleted from the database, resulting in direct loss of funds evidence and inability to recover disputed transfers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged user craft a record item whose consensusTimestamp equals Long.MAX_VALUE so that the JPQL query 'delete from ContractResult where consensusTimestamp <= ?1' deletes every ContractResult row in the database, erasing all on-chain fund-transfer audit trails and making fund recovery impossible?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the absence of a lower-bound guard in prune() to pass consensusTimestamp=0, causing the query 'delete from ContractResult where consensusTimestamp <= 0' to silently succeed and delete genesis-era contract results that record initial fund allocations, leading to unrecoverable loss of fund provenance?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Given that prune() is annotated @Transactional and @Modifying with no row-count validation, can an unprivileged user race a legitimate high-value contract call so that prune() executes concurrently and deletes the ContractResult for that call before it is committed, causing the fund transfer to be unrecorded and the funds effectively lost?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged user submit a stream of transactions with artificially inflated consensusTimestamp values to advance the retention window, causing prune() to delete ContractResult rows for still-pending fund transfers whose settlement depends on those records, resulting in direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Since prune() returns an int (rows deleted) that is never checked by callers, can an unprivileged user trigger a bulk deletion of ContractResult rows representing high-value transfers without any alerting or rollback mechanism, making the deletion silent and the fund loss undetectable?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that prune() uses a raw long parameter with no type-safe wrapper or range validation to pass a negative consensusTimestamp (e.g., -1), causing undefined SQL behavior or deleting records with negative timestamps that represent special fund-transfer events?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged user who controls the content of a submitted Ethereum transaction manipulate the consensusTimestamp assigned to their ContractResult record so that it falls below the prune threshold, causing their own high-value transaction record to be pruned before a counterparty can verify receipt of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Given that ContractResultRepository extends CrudRepository and prune() is the only deletion method, can an unprivileged user exploit a missing authorization check on the prune() invocation path to call it directly via an exposed REST or gRPC endpoint, deleting ContractResult records for active fund transfers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged user exploit a time-of-check/time-of-use race between the retention period calculation and the prune() execution to delete ContractResult records for transactions that are still within the retention window, causing loss of fund-transfer records that users rely on for dispute resolution?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged user submit a malformed record file that causes the importer to compute an incorrect consensusTimestamp for a ContractResult, placing it below the prune threshold immediately upon insertion, so that the next prune() call deletes the record before any fund settlement is confirmed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Since the JPQL query in prune() uses a positional parameter (?1) with no additional WHERE clause constraints, can an unprivileged user exploit a parameter injection vulnerability in the consensusTimestamp value to broaden the deletion predicate and delete ContractResult rows outside the intended retention window, erasing fund-transfer records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the lack of a minimum-retention guard in prune() to trigger deletion of ContractResult records for transactions that are still referenced by open payment channels or escrow contracts, causing those contracts to malfunction and lock or lose funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged user submit a high volume of low-value contract calls with timestamps just above the prune threshold to exhaust database storage, forcing an emergency prune() with an elevated consensusTimestamp that deletes ContractResult records for legitimate high-value transfers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Given that prune() is @Transactional but there is no distributed lock, can an unprivileged user trigger two concurrent prune() calls with different consensusTimestamp values, causing a race condition that deletes more ContractResult rows than intended and erases fund-transfer records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that prune() deletes by consensusTimestamp (the primary key / ID field) to craft a transaction whose consensusTimestamp collides with a high-value existing ContractResult, causing the prune() call to delete that record and erase evidence of the fund transfer?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the absence of a foreign-key cascade check in prune() to delete ContractResult rows that are referenced by ContractTransactionHash records, causing referential integrity violations that corrupt the fund-transfer lookup index and make funds unrecoverable?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the @Modifying annotation's default clearAutomatically=false behavior so that after prune() executes, the first-level Hibernate cache still holds stale ContractResult entities, causing subsequent reads to return deleted fund-transfer records and mislead downstream fund-settlement logic?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that prune() does not emit any domain event or audit log entry upon deletion to silently erase ContractResult records for their own fraudulent transactions, preventing detection of the fund manipulation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged user exploit a clock skew between the consensus node and the importer to cause prune() to receive a consensusTimestamp that is ahead of real time, deleting ContractResult records for transactions that have not yet been fully settled, resulting in direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged user submit a transaction that causes the importer to call prune() with a consensusTimestamp derived from an attacker-controlled field (e.g., a manipulated record file header), allowing the attacker to set the deletion threshold arbitrarily and erase ContractResult records for high-value transfers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the lack of idempotency protection in prune() to replay the same prune request multiple times with an incrementally increasing consensusTimestamp, progressively deleting more ContractResult records until all fund-transfer history is erased?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that prune() operates on the consensusTimestamp primary key (which is also the entity ID per getId()) to delete a ContractResult record whose consensusTimestamp matches a known high-value transfer, causing that transfer to be unrecorded and the funds lost?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the absence of a minimum-rows-retained constraint in prune() to delete all ContractResult rows in a single call, causing the mirror node to report zero contract executions and making all fund transfers appear to have never occurred?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged user exploit a deserialization vulnerability in the consensusTimestamp parameter path to inject a Long.MAX_VALUE value into prune(), causing the JPQL delete to match every ContractResult row and permanently erase all fund-transfer records?
